# tokens: # inspect — machine-readable introspection of words, values, and types
# `inspect X` returns an `Inspect` value — a Map (`Node/Map/Inspect`)
#   inspect WORD   — a bare word: looked up (type stack, then def stack,
#                    then registered word). Returns a *word inspection*
#                    for a registered word, or a *type inspection* for a
#   inspect ATOM   — `( quote name )`: the atom is treated as a NAME and
#   inspect V      — anything else (a concrete value, a type literal, a
#                    record / object / disjunct / enum / typed list / typed
#                    map / fn-shape value): returns its *type inspection*.
# The result carries VType `Inspect` (a subtype of `Map`) but its
# payload is an OrderedMap, so it renders comma-separated (`{k:v,k:v}`)
# Two footguns: a bare word like `true` is inspected as the NAME 'true'
# (unknown), not the boolean value — put it on the stack first / wrap
# it in `( … )` for the value. Likewise `( quote red )` inspects the
# --- a fixture word (forward-prec, one [Number Number] sig) ---
# 2. inspect — builtin type literals (a type inspection; kind:literal)
# Every type-name word, used as a value, reports `kind:literal` with
# `type` = its leaf name. (`FunctionSignature` is the exception — its
# VType is recognised as a function-shape, so it reports
# --- scalars: kind:literal, type = the value's exact VType leaf ---
# --- a bare word / atom is inspected as a NAME, not as a value ---

note: the WORD 'true' isn't registered — not the boolean value
the atom is treated as a name to look up

# tokens: # 4. inspect — type VALUES (record / object / disjunct / enum / fn-shape)
# Constructed inline with parens, or named with `type NAME …` first
# (naming adds a `name:` field). Anonymous `object {…}` has a
# non-deterministic internal ID, so it's only shown via a named type.
type Pt record [ { x:Integer } { y:Integer } ] inspect Pt
# --- object (kind:object; fields includes inherited, parent first) ---
type Vec object { x:Integer y:Integer } inspect Vec
type Bar object { b:Integer } type Baz object { c:String } Bar inspect Baz
# --- disjunct / enum (alternatives are the members' VType paths) ---
type D ( Integer tor Boolean ) inspect D
type Color enum [ red green blue ] inspect Color
inspect ( fnsig [ [ Integer ] [ Integer ] [ String ] [ String ] ] )
type Sig fnsig [ [ Integer ] [ Integer ] ] inspect Sig
# --- a function VALUE / typed-list / typed-map / alias named type ---

note: a function value reports literal — the fnsig form carries the shape
an alias of a builtin — literal

# tokens: # 5. inspect — def'd values, unknown names, and inspect-of-inspect

note: a def'd plain value — kind:defined, no signatures

# tokens: def g fn [ [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a addq 1 ] ] inspect g
note: an fn-defined word — buildInspection sees only the compiled dispatch table, so kind:native
not a type, def, or word
an inspect result is an `Inspect` value
inspecting an inspect result — a plain Inspect-typed map
